concerns.text: FAO STAT provides aggregate country-level agricultural statistics, NOT individual farmer survey data with community engagement items. World Bank LSMS surveys may have some variables but the spec conflates these sources. This makes the research design infeasible as specified.

SC-003 requires AUC ≥ 0.70 as a success criterion. This creates circular validation where study 'success' depends on achieving model performance thresholds, not on whether the research question was answered. Null results should not be treated as failures.

Mediation analysis using Baron & Kenny approach in observational data makes strong causal assumptions (no unmeasured confounding) that are rarely met. Bootstrap CI helps but doesn't solve the fundamental identification problem in non-experimental data.

SC-002 requires Cronbach's α ≥ 0.70 for engagement score. With proxy variables from available datasets, this threshold may not be achievable, creating a situation where scientifically valid work 'fails' due to data limitations rather than research quality.

Bonferroni correction may be overly conservative for exploratory agricultural survey research with 5 predictors. Consider false discovery rate (FDR) control instead, which is more appropriate for this context.
